entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) || Part 6 || Configuring Mail Adapter
entry.content: great blog, appreciated

entry.title: Change data in the dropdown list or searchHelp with draft in RAP in Odata v4gets the old text again
entry.content:
In Edit mode of an instance with draft in RAP in Odata , I am trying to change the data on dropdown list or in the search help it gets back the old text ( this issue not happens in Odata v2 ) .

ex : its selected already as 'Forum' when i am trying to change it to 'Workshop'  the text gets back to 'Forum' again but if i saved the data it makes it correct as 'Workshop'  . 

 

Can any one help ?

entry.title: Re: RAP : Trying to make a child with composition relatio...
entry.content: @ShivamChadha if you can help in this blog : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/change-data-in-the-dropdown-list-or-searchhelp-with-draft-in-rap-in-odata/qaq-p/14087575#M4911996

entry.title: Re: How to extract MBC Bank Statement file data from SAP...
entry.content: I am sorry but the last part with the Bank Statement SOAP API is simply not correct. The API does not deal with any CGI standard format. It's an API with own fields. This answer is not correct here.

entry.title: Re: Preferred unit of measure
entry.content: Hi, I have maintained the Warehouse Attribute= BAG. If ERP inbound delivery 48 pc -> inbound delivery in WM 4 bags -> warehouse task still 1 PAL. If ERP inbound delivery 24 pcs -> WM inbound delivery 2 BAG -> WT 2 BAGs. I would expect AuM is BAG always. Why it is not?

entry.title: Re: How to extract MBC Bank Statement file data from SAP...
entry.content:
Hi munipkumar,

 

what you are looking for is something like the SAP Advanced Payment Management. They deal and forward messages using the CGI formats like the CAMT formats.

 

Read more into this: https://www.sap.com/germany/products/financial-management/advanced-payment-management.html

 

What was proposed and accepted as solution in this thread, is not completely correct. Especially the last part with the SOAP API doesn't fit to your requirements. You would need to map your CAMT file to the API fields, which is I would say some unnecessary work.

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: Re: List of banks involved in Multibank Connectivity
entry.content: there is no list of member banks provided in that Note!

entry.title: Re: Exactly once in order (EOIO) for Group messages using...
entry.content: Well, the moment you move a message to DMQ, the sequence is broken. The order is no longer guaranteed as the original queue processing continues based on its own setup and the DMQ messages gets processed on its own setup. It is a tricky situation. The options I see is, group the messages into respective queues where EOIO has to be maintained and then from there, process the messages without moving them to DMQ in case of temporary failures. But at the end, if you move the message to DMQ, you have to compromise on the sequence, as far as I see.

entry.title: Re: How many child tables are added to UDO
entry.content: How can I register 20 tables in a single UDO by using c#

entry.title: Re: Monitor Bank Fees app Camt.086 AFP service Service Codes calculated volume field
entry.content:
Hi Emma,

We are having the same issues in our client. I have maintained the service code in OT83, and with and without assigning service in the Manage Bank Fees app, it is not working. I mean, the calculated volume field is not getting updated. We did the custom transformation as suggested by SAP, but still.

Here is the sample list. Can you elaborate how you resolved the issue?

  •  


Service IDService Common Code IssuerService Common CodeService Type00099 (DEPOSIT BANK ASSESSMENT)AFP000230 01005 (INTEREST BEARING ACCOUNT MAINT)AFP010010 01564 (CASHPRO CONNECT PDR ACCT)AFP400002 01565 (CASHPRO CONNECT PDR ITEM)AFP400222 01566 (CASHPRO CONNECT CDR ACCT)AFP400005 01567 (CASHPRO CONNECT CDR ITEM)AFP400225 01655 (CASHPRO ONLINE SUBSCRIPTION)AFP40027Z 01701 (WIRE MONTHLY SUBSCRIPTION)AFP350000 01720 (CASHPRO REPORTING SUBSCRIPTION)AFP40027Z 01721 (CASHPRO ONLINE PDR ACCOUNT)AFP400052 01724 (CASHPRO ONLINE PDR ITEM)AFP400272 01743 (CASHPRO ONLINE CDR ACCOUNT)AFP400055 01744 (CASHPRO ONLINE CDR ITEM)AFP400275 08529 (ELEC WIRE OUT-DOMESTIC)AFP350103 17554 (CASHPRO CONNECT PMTS PER ITEM)AFP330020 17555 (CASHPRO CONNECT PMTS PER FILE)AFP330010 19525 (CASHPRO CONNECT PMTS MAINT)AFP330000 19761 (ACH BLOCKS AUTH INSTRUCTIONS)AFP251057 20365 (CASHPRO GP CUST MNT TMPLT STRG)AFP350551 44332 (CASHPRO IMAGE SUBSCRIPTION)AFP151350 45042 (ACH MONTHLY MAINTENANCE)AFP250000 45076 (ACH BLOCKS AUTH MAINTENANCE)AFP251050 82003 (DEBITS POSTED-OTHER)AFP010100 82004 (CREDITS POSTED-OTHER)AFP010101 99970 (EDI SERVICES CR ADJ)AFP999999

entry.title: No data entry profile could be determined for CATS
entry.content:
Hello All, 

I am not using CATS, and has no intention as well. In this project we dont have plan to implement CATS and HR module in S4hana as customer is using other system for time card and HR things. 

While creating service Confirmation from a Service Order, I am getting error as "No data entry profile could be determined for CATS". How to resolve this. 

Thanks 

Bidhu

entry.title: Create and use Service teams without creating HR data for team owner or member
entry.content:
Hello all,

How can I create and use Service teams without creating HR data for team owner or member. We dont have HR module in our Project. To create a service team in Responsibility management, (We use Enterprise Organization Management) , system asking for a business partner with User ID and that need to be linked HR master data. Or I am understanding wrong. 

THanks 

Bidhu

entry.title: Re: Fiori-Style Analytics Dashboard: 'Invalid Username/Pa...
entry.content:
the problem here you have is because you are login with sap business one user in upper cases users like GG or mixed cases like Gg , this is problem is not showing with users who login with lower case users , this is due to to a problem with keycloak authentication which is converting any user to lower case and because of course it is hana , it is case sensitive the fiori cockpit will reject the login becuase of this issue , i have developed jar file which is Custom user storage in the keycloak will override the default configuration of keycloak and ignore the auto conversion of the user sessions to lower case .. what you need to do is download this jar file

https://github.com/maenmustafa/keycloak/blob/main/ANG_custom-userstorage-1.0.0.jar 

and place it in :

/usr/sap/SAPBusinessOne/Common/keycloak/providers/
then give the correct authorizations : 
chown b1service0:b1service0 ANG_custom-userstorage-1.0.0.jar
chmod 776 ANG_custom-userstorage-1.0.0.jar

now restart authentication service :

service sapb1servertools-authentication restart

then login to sapb1 realm

https://ServerIP_Or_Name:40020/auth/admin/sapb1/console/ 

login with B1SiteUser , then go to User Federation --> Add Customer-user-Storage providers

give it any name like CaseSensitiveUserName  , and set Cache Policy --> Default.

then you will see tail name CaseSenseitiveUserName or the name of the provider you can .. click on the name 

then from the action menu --> sync all users.

 

login to sap business one with the target user and the problem will be solved.

Regards

Maen AbuTabanjeh

entry.title: Re: Removing horizontal scroll bar from SAP GUI Screen
entry.content: Hi, thank you Sander Rossi for reply. How can i know which creen is being called or used in my scenario, because I am not aware which screen is getting called. Is there any way to check it?

entry.title: How to import master data from Datasphere to SAC
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I would like to create a new Public dimension in SAC and import the Master data (Attributes, Texts and Hierarchies) to the SAC dimension.

Please let me know if it is possible. Any detailed steps/guide to configure would be of great help.

Thanks and Regards,

Venkat

entry.title: Re: PO Quantity check control in MIRO while posting Custo...
entry.content:
Hi,

I would suggest to set the tolerance in the configuration equal 0 in below tolerance keys. Unfortunatelly i cannot tell you which suits you best, so you need to play around with them.

You can find this configuration in the following path:

Procurement => Invoice Management => Supplier Invoice and Credit Memos => Incoming Invoice => Set Tolerance Limits

If you will have more questions please let me know.

Thank you 

Dariusz Kalmarczyk

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Salvador 2025: A Celebration of Innovation, Community, and the Future of SAP
entry.content:
Salvador, with its rich history as the first capital of Brazil, is a city renowned for its vibrant culture, stunning colonial architecture, and profound roots in shaping Brazil’s national identity. Just as Salvador’s history of blending influences to create something unique, this SAP Inside Track event brought together diverse minds to discuss the future of enterprise technology, share success stories, and inspire one another.

The event was packed with insightful lectures and an enthusiastic audience, showing that the SAP community in Brazil is stronger than ever. Let’s take a look at the highlights:

Marssel Vilaça: The Power of the SAP Community

Marssel opened the day by reminding everyone of the incredible strength that lies within the SAP Community. He emphasized how collaboration, knowledge sharing, and active community participation can elevate careers and projects. His inspiring words set the perfect tone for the day.

Fabio Dezan: The New Brazil Tax Legislation and SAP Challenges

Fabio tackled one of the most pressing topics: the recent updates to Brazil’s complex tax legislation. He shared practical challenges and insights on how SAP systems, especially S/4HANA, must adapt to comply with these new rules. It was a highly technical yet accessible session that added significant value for consultants and customers alike.

Aline Damasceno: Stocks Management Using S/4HANA

Aline delivered an excellent lecture focused on optimizing stock and inventory management processes within S/4HANA. She demonstrated how modern functionalities can bring visibility, agility, and efficiency to supply chain operations —a crucial topic for industries facing volatile demand and supply conditions.

Manuel Robalinho(me): Finance APIs and Gen AI for Cash Flow

Manuel’s session was one of the most anticipated. He explained how Finance APIs can streamline integrations for Accounts Receivable, Accounts Payable, and Bank Statement processing with Brazilian banks. He also brought a futuristic angle by presenting how Generative AI can predict and optimize cash flow operations — a real glimpse into the future of finance!

Illiada Campos: PM Module Success Story

Illiada captivated everyone with a fantastic case study combining standard SAP functionalities with the Plant Maintenance (PM) Module. Her presentation showed how a strategic use of what’s already available in SAP can achieve massive project success without excessive customization — a key Clean Core principle.

Elianna Lanna: S/4HANA Greenfield at PetroRecôncavo

Elianna gave a powerful presentation about the Greenfield implementation of S/4HANA at PetroRecôncavo. She walked the audience through the project's major challenges, including change management, data migration, and aligning business processes with the new system, showing the resilience and adaptability needed for large-scale transformations.

Luciano Casado: Joule and S/4HANA Public Cloud

Luciano impressed the audience by showing the integration of Joule, SAP’s Business AI, with S/4HANA Public Cloud. His lecture demonstrated how AI is not just a trend, but a tool to significantly improve decision-making and automate routine tasks, providing a real competitive advantage.

Lucas Ferreira: Fiori and Clean Core Using Tachyonix

Lucas gave a masterclass on the importance of the Clean Core strategy and how Fiori apps can be customized efficiently using the Tachyonix product. He emphasized the importance of minimizing modifications to maintain the system's scalability and upgradability, a message that resonated strongly with the audience.

Otto: SAP Accelerate by Accenture

Otto closed the lectures with a dynamic talk about SAP Accelerate, a product developed by Accenture to speed up SAP project deliveries while maintaining quality. His lecture showed real cases where time-to-value was significantly improved, impressing everyone with practical results.

An Unforgettable Audience

The event was marked by a fantastic turnout — a true testament to the passion of the SAP community in Salvador and Brazil. Participants were highly engaged, asking questions, sharing experiences, and demonstrating that collaboration and knowledge sharing are alive and well.

There was an increase in female participation in the lectures at this SIT. Fifty percent of the lectures were presented by SAP consultants who shared their success stories. Amazing!

See You in 2026!

Given the success of this year’s event, the organizers announced that SAP Inside Track Salvador will return in 2026, even bigger and better. The goal is to continue building this community, expanding its topics, and introducing even more innovation and collaboration to the SAP ecosystem in Brazil.

Congratulations to everyone involved — speakers, participants, organizers, and sponsors — for making SAP Inside Track Salvador 2025 an unforgettable experience!

#SAPCommunity #SAPInsideTrack #SITSAL #SAPS4HANA #SAPFiori #CleanCore #FinanceAPIs #GenAI #SAPPublicCloud #SAPAccelerate  #BrazilTaxUpdate

entry.title: Re: Removing horizontal scroll bar from SAP GUI Screen
entry.content: Search in the forum how to create a SAP GUI Shortcut to run "/h" for popups (/h to start the debugger). Note that if it's a STANDARD popup screen, it seems to be a waste of time to remove the horizontal scrollbar, I guess you can work on more important topics.

entry.title: Re: Mapas Fiscais Portugal - 33 - Reintegrações - Fiscal...
entry.content:
Hello @Crocha ,

Thank you for your question.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, regarding Portugal localisation, Fiscal Map 33 (associated with asset revaluations and depreciations) is generated only when the asset master record has the requisite tax-relevant fields completed (e.g., "Fiscal Revaluation Allowed") and the revaluation is correctly posted using the legal revaluation transaction.

Checklist to resolve:

  • Ensure the asset master data includes all mandatory localisation fields (Portugal-specific fields).
  • Verify that the revaluation was posted using the correct Portuguese-specific revaluation process (not merely a general revaluation).
  • Confirm that the depreciation posting (AFAB or equivalent) was executed following the revaluation.
  • Ensure that the Portugal-specific reporting configuration is active in the system (scope item 2W2 - Financial Closing and Reporting for Portugal).

If any of these steps are absent, the Fiscal Map 33 will not generate.

Let me know if you need further assistance

Best regards,
Chuma

entry.title: Can sap public cloud Warehouse management b e integrated with Sap warehouse robotics ? AMR / AGV int
entry.content: Sap public cloud warehouse mgt integrating with Sap warehouse robotics to connect or operate AMR/AGV (automated warehouse retrieval or automated guided vehicles ) has any one implemented this to understand the architecture?

entry.title: Re: Supplier Inovice line item split for two VAT codes
entry.content:
Hi,

Just to sum up. You have a PO with 1 line item and in price conditions you have a customs fee, and during invoicing in MIRO you would like to have 2 line items with different taxes. 

Please try to do as follows:

In MIRO please change the view from "Goods/service items" to "Goods/services items + planned delivery costs" (you might need to click ENTER so the view will change). Than in line items you will get 2 lines. First the line from PO and second from the condition. You can set two different tax codes on each line, which will be visible during simulation and posting:

If you have any question please let me know.

Dariusz Kalmarczyk

entry.title: Custom Tile Visualization & Navigation in SAP Build Workzone - Cloud foundry launchpad: Solved
entry.content:
Introduction -

SAP Build Workzone is a powerful platform for modern enterprise applications. However, during my development journey, I encountered a significant hurdle: The standard offering does not currently provide a built-in option for creating custom visualizations.  While I initially relied on official documentation and community blogs, I discovered that these resources were insufficient or focused on SAP Fiori Launchpad or SAP Cloud Portal service, etc. This blog details my approach and the workarounds I developed to bridge the gap.

The Challenge -

Overcoming a Lack of option to implement Custom visualization. It left developers like me to carve our own path in delivering the tailored user experiences our projects demanded.

Official Documentation -

This is the only official documentation available in help.sap.com which aims to explain how to implement a custom visualization. But, as it only speaks moderately about the manifests of both the file, it becomes tough to implement the solution.

A glance at the custom tile -

Both the standard tile types provided by SAP offers only basic configurable fields like title, subtitle, icon, and description. Additionally, the dynamic tile can display a single value (fetching dynamically from backend service). But both of them lacks in customizing the tile's visualization.

The 1x2 tile above is custom implemented. Its loading a custom visualization and when clicked, it navigates to the main/target application within the launchpad with personalized site settings (as expected from a launchpad tile). Below is the target app that launches on click of the custom tile. By the end of the blog, you too would be able to achieve this for your app.

Let's dive into the solution -

You need to build two apps with different IDs. One for the visualization itself and one that implements the visualization. If you already have your [target] app in your Build Workzone, you can modify its manifest and re-deploy along with its visualization app.

Custom Visualization App: 

This app will have the visual content to load on the target app's tile. The trick is in the app's controller and the manifest, where we take care of the navigation to the target app and launchpad configs.

manifest.json

The crucial section of manifest.json is sap.flp, which tells the app should be considered by the launchpad as a tile and of size 1x2.

{
    "_version": "1.65.0",
    "sap.app": {
        "id": "customtileviz",
        "type": "application",
        "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
        "applicationVersion": {
            "version": "0.0.1"
        },
        "title": "{{appTitle}}",
        "description": "{{appDescription}}",
        "resources": "resources.json"
    },
    "sap.ui": {
        "technology": "UI5",
        "icons": {
            "icon": "",
            "favIcon": "",
            "phone": "",
            "phone@2": "",
            "tablet": "",
            "tablet@2": ""
        },
        "deviceTypes": {
            "desktop": true,
            "tablet": true,
            "phone": true
        }
    },
    "sap.ui5": {
        "flexEnabled": false,
        "dependencies": {
            "minUI5Version": "1.132.1",
            "libs": {
                "sap.m": {},
                "sap.ui.core": {}
            }
        },
        "contentDensities": {
            "compact": true,
            "cozy": true
        },
        "models": {
            "i18n": {
                "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
                "settings": {
                    "bundleName": "customtileviz.i18n.i18n"
                }
            }
        },
        "resources": {
            "css": [
                {
                    "uri": "css/style.css"
                }
            ]
        },
        "rootView": {
            "viewName": "customtileviz.view.MainView",
            "type": "XML",
            "id": "customtileviz"
        }
    },
    "sap.cloud": {
        "public": true,
        "service": "customtileviz"
    },
    "sap.flp": {
        "type": "tile",
        "tileSize": "1x2"
    }
}

 

MainView.controller.js

In the visualization app's controller (shown below), I'm using the sap.ushell.Container.getServiceAsyn to use its CrossApplicationNavigation service for navigating to the target app. The shell hash takes the values of semantic object and action that is defined within the target app's manifest file.

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller"
], (Controller) => {
    "use strict";

    return Controller.extend("customtileviz.controller.MainView", {
        onInit: function () {
            const oView = this.getView();
      
            oView.addEventDelegate({
              onAfterRendering: () => {
                const mainDom = oView.getDomRef();
                if (mainDom) {
                  mainDom.addEventListener("click", () => {
                    this.navigateToAnotherApp();
                  });
                }
              }
            });
          },
      
          navigateToAnotherApp: async function () {
            try {
              const oCrossAppNav = await sap.ushell.Container.getServiceAsync("CrossApplicationNavigation");
              const sHash = "targetapp-display"; // Replace with you target app's semantic object and action
          
              oCrossAppNav.toExternal({
                target: {
                  shellHash: sHash
                }
              });
            } catch (err) {
              console.error("Navigation service unavailable:", err);
            }
          },
    });
});

 

Target App: App that you want to launch from the tile

Manifest.json of the target app is crucial where you need to set the app's intent for cross-navigation and set the visualization properties of the app. Apart from the manifest we do not need to take care of any other files in the target app.

manifest.json

{
  "_version": "1.65.0",
  "sap.app": {
    "id": "targetapp",
    "type": "application",
    "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
    "applicationVersion": {
      "version": "0.0.1"
    },
    "title": "{{appTitle}}",
    "description": "{{appDescription}}",
    "crossNavigation": {
      "inbounds": {
        "intent1": {
          "semanticObject": "targetapp",
          "action": "display",
          "title": "Target App",
          "subTitle": "{{flpSubtitle}}",
          "signature": {
            "parameters": {},
            "additionalParameters": "allowed"
          }
        }
      }
    },
    "resources": "resources.json",
    "sourceTemplate": {
      "id": "@sap/generator-fiori:basic",
      "version": "1.17.3",
      "toolsId": "69ae75e3-85b4-4ffb-bfd8-b73b13f30c65"
    }
  },
  "sap.ui": {
    "technology": "UI5",
    "icons": {
      "icon": "",
      "favIcon": "",
      "phone": "",
      "phone@2": "",
      "tablet": "",
      "tablet@2": ""
    },
    "deviceTypes": {
      "desktop": true,
      "tablet": true,
      "phone": true
    }
  },
  "sap.ui5": {
    "flexEnabled": false,
    "dependencies": {
      "minUI5Version": "1.135.0",
      "libs": {
        "sap.m": {},
        "sap.ui.core": {}
      }
    },
    "contentDensities": {
      "compact": true,
      "cozy": true
    },
    "models": {
      "i18n": {
        "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
        "settings": {
          "bundleName": "targetapp.i18n.i18n"
        }
      }
    },
    "resources": {
      "css": [
        {
          "uri": "css/style.css"
        }
      ]
    },
    "routing": {
      "config": {
        "routerClass": "sap.m.routing.Router",
        "controlAggregation": "pages",
        "controlId": "app",
        "transition": "slide",
        "type": "View",
        "viewType": "XML",
        "path": "targetapp.view",
        "async": true,
        "viewPath": "targetapp.view"
      },
      "routes": [
        {
          "name": "RouteDynamicPageAnalyticalTable",
          "pattern": ":?query:",
          "target": [
            "TargetDynamicPageAnalyticalTable"
          ]
        }
      ],
      "targets": {
        "TargetDynamicPageAnalyticalTable": {
          "id": "DynamicPageAnalyticalTable",
          "name": "DynamicPageAnalyticalTable"
        }
      }
    },
    "rootView": {
      "viewName": "targetapp.view.App",
      "type": "XML",
      "id": "App",
      "async": true
    }
  },
  "sap.platform.hcp": {
    "uri": "",
    "appName": "targetapp",
    "_version": "1.1.0"
  },
  "sap.cloud": {
    "public": true,
    "service": "targetapp"
  },
  "sap.cloud.portal": {
    "targetapp-display": {
      "vizType": "customtileviz"
    }
  }
}

Changes you need to make in the target app's manifest -

1. In "crossNavigation" section I've set the app's intent i.e., semantic object and action. You have to use this same combination in the controller of the visualization app to enable the launchpad navigation to the target app. You cannot test this in preview as this service would be available only when the app loads in the launchpad.

2. In "sap.cloud.portal" I've used the same combination of "semantic object-action" to define this app's visualization to be loaded from the custom tile app. In the vizType property I have mentioned the app id of the visualization app.

Final steps:

Once you've maintained these configs in respective apps, you have to deploy them in your Cloud foundry environment/space. On successful deployment, refresh the contents in Build Workzone's Channel manager. You will only see the Target app in the content manager. The visualization app wouldn't appear in the launchpad's content manager. Do not worry! you just need to add the target app to the required role and group. And voila!! it's that simple to implement a custom visualization.

Exception and fix -

If you're using a backend service to load data in your visualization, you might get to see the tile loading with an error saying, "cannot load tile".

In that case, go to your site settings from the site directory in Build Workzone.

Under Browser settings, set the Asynchronous Module Loading as No (shown above). This should fix the tile loading issue. The same error could appear if your app has some bugs. Advising you to run the app locally to preview content before deploying.

Conclusion -

While the standard option to create custom visualizations remains unavailable, End users who are transitioning to the Cloud foundry environment are missing out on the custom tile feature they had in Neo environment. I hope this implementation helps in resolving the visualization challenges faced while migrating from Neo to Cloud foundry environment and also helps them who are just exploring the launchpad capabilities.

By tweaking this implementation, it's possible to have a custom tile to launch a website/URL in a new tab outside of the launchpad. It's also possible to just load quick links on the custom tile and it's possible too just to load visualization on a tile without any action on Click. Keep exploring and holler if you need any help or want to share feedback.

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Salvador 2025: A Celebration of Innovation, Community, and the Future of SAP
entry.content:
O SITSAL 2025 foi maravilhoso, muita gratidão a todos os envolvidos, ao tempo da organização: Manoela Magalhães, Núbia Lima, Leonardo Portugal, Iran, João Vitor, Carol Lolis, Marcos Oliveira, Eduardo Palafoz, Michelle Batista, Leo Coelho, Roberta Fernandes.. agradecimento aos palestrantes que organizaram esse evento tão rico: Manuel Robalinho, Ilíada Campos, Eliana Lanna, Lucas Ferreira, Fabio Dezan, Luciano Casado e Marssel Vilaça. 

Como falamos aqui na Bahia, foi muito TOP, a cada momento, cada palestra, tivemos uma sala para 100 pessoas, mudamos de sala, aumentamos para 120 e tivemos 135 participantes. Um registro público para Salvador.

Agora vamos fortalecer nossa comunidade!

Obrigada mais uma vez a todos!

entry.title: Re: SES done with wrong Qty and unusable to reverse as in...
entry.content:
Hello,

Unfortunately the only way to do it is to cancel the invoice. Then you will be able to revoke the approval and correct the quantity.

If you need any clarification please let me know.

Best regards

Dariusz Kalmarczyk

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK - search filter does not work for some letter...
entry.content: Thank you. It worked. I set Database Collation: 1250LATIN2.

entry.title: Re: Is there something for user tolerance for accepting p...
entry.content:
Hi,

The easiest way to achieve a solution for your case is to:

- Create a new movement type (for example Z01).

- Switch off SLED check for the new movement type.

- Give only supervisiors access to the new movement type.

After that during performing the GR supervisor will be able to use this new movement type to perform GR of goods with exeeded minimum remaining shelf life days.

New movement type you can configure:

Logistics, Transportation, and Warehousing => Inventory Management = > Core Inventory => Copy of Movement Type

Switch of SLED for specific movement type:

Logistics, Transportation, and Warehousing => Inventory Management = > Core Inventory => Set Expiration Date Check 

If you need any other help please let me know.

Best regards

Dariusz Kalmarczyk

entry.title: Vulnerabilities and Compromises
entry.content:
Over this past weekend I saw 2 social media posts on a "zero-day" vulnerability. As usual with bad news, it dropped when many people may not be on the office network. I am curious who has seen this news, how they found it, and whether they escalated/responded. Don't post with "we're on X version" or whatever, just if you knew/know.

"SAP has released an out-of-band emergency update to fix a critical zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2025-31324) in NetWeaver Visual Composer — and it’s already being exploited in the wild." [source: https://infosec.exchange/@Efani/114405113950458290 ]

See: SAP Security Note 3594142

entry.title: Re: SEN Kyma migration_datacenter
entry.content:
Hi Maggy,

Unfortunately there is no complete schedule of when individual customers will be migrated.  Enable Now customer contacts will receive an email notification 30 days before their migration, which triggers the migration process.  They will then receive a second email about 7 days before the migration to confirm the specific date of their migration.  The key is to ensure that the people registered to receive SAP Enable Now notifications in SAP for Me are aware of these notifications and ensure that they relevant people in your organization are notified when they are received.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: SSO ACCESS ERROR ENABLE NOW
entry.content:
Hi Hugo,

You must log in using a manual Enable Now user account.  If you have no manual user account, please use the 'admin' account that was provisioned with your system.  If you do not know the password for the 'admin' account, please raise a support ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-MGR and ask to have the password for the 'admin' account reset.  The new password will be emailed to the people registered to receive Enable Now notifications in SAP for Me. 

Once you have the new password, you can access the manual login screen by adding /formlogin/ to the end of your normal Enable Now URL.  Adding /formlogin/ bypasses SSO.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Unable to launch or play the existing course book or...
entry.content:
Hi there,

Please raise a support ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-MGR and get support involved in this issue.  It looks like you have an issue with your content structure but it's different to say what is wrong without taking a closer look.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Bubble text window not displaying properly
entry.content:
Hi there,

I have not received any other notifications of this issue so I would restart your laptop and Producer and if the issue persists please raise a support ticket using the Component ID KM-SNE-PRO and get support involved.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Migration EC-CS to GR and Mapping
entry.content:
Hi Eyal,

When migrating from EC-CS to Group Reporting, the hierarchy you maintained in CX17 (Cons COA) won’t be used directly anymore.
In Group Reporting, hierarchy management is handled exclusively through the Manage Global Hierarchies app and FS Item configuration.

You'll need to replicate or rebuild your existing CX17 hierarchy inside Group Reporting using:

  • Define FS Items (for FS Item creation)

  • Manage Global Hierarchies (for hierarchy structure)

  • Mapping GL accounts to FS Items (to link your operational COA)

Unfortunately, there’s no automated transfer from CX17, it’s a manual alignment during migration.

Hope this helps you plan it more smoothly

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Link of Subcon Challan with PO in S/4HANA public cloud

entry.title: Re: 收入認列錯誤
entry.content:
您好，

收入确认与实际开票可以理解为并行的动作。收入确认并不会影响实际开票，也不会与实际开票相互转换。可以理解为收入确认仅仅是在项目运行过程中对收入情况的一个预估和把控。正常在项目关闭时候所有收入确认科目就会都blance掉的。

Best Regards,
Owen

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Need APIs for Automating F...
entry.content:
Hello,

The best way to find API is from the API Hub:

ODATA V2 API | SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

You can try to find in each tab.

If none exists, that means no existing API.

You need to raise feature request.

Best Regards,
Owen

entry.title: Re: How to Map Parent-Child Relationship for Both Customers & Vendors in SAP S/4HANA Public Clou
entry.content: Informed other experts again.

entry.title: Re: Exactly once in order (EOIO) for Group messages using...
entry.content:
Thanks a lot for your response Mani (sorry if I get your name wrong). Are you planning to have dedicated queues for each Work Order?

I know we have option of Temporary Queues, but that doesn't guarantee the Delivery.

I was thinking this:

  1. Publisher will publish the messages to one queue (Exclusive Queue) as normal
  2. The Subscriber (SCPI in our case) will first query the database to see if this new message belongs of an Order that has failed in the past. We will use composite key of the key fields that the Order to identity the order. If the Order is found in the DB, then this message will not be continued and will be inserted to the DB (or also pushed to the DMQ).
  3. I am also thinking to create two tables - one will only hold the order number and time of arrival, and other will hold the complete payload. This way searching Order messages using Table 1 (metadata) would become easier.

HANA Cloud Database and DMQ in sycn

Since we are dealing with two data sources for failures, it’s important to make sure that both are in sync.

One way of doing it is to forward DMQed message to HANA DB Table as soon as it enters DMQ. This way DMQed messages will be available in HANA DB at All times.

The HANA DB Table will be queried before continuing the process of sending the message to a Receiver

Owen

entry.title: CCLM numbers in SAP for ME - Custom Code Analytics
entry.content:
We need to report to the executive team (C-level) of the company the updated numbers for Custom Code Management. Previously, we used the CCLM report within Solman, but after migrating to RISE, we were advised to use the Custom Code Analytics report within "System and Provisioning."

 

Currently, the numbers we have for objects * and the namespace registered for the company do not match the numbers presented in SAP4ME.

 

We request support for:

  1. Understanding the new dashboards and how the calculations for Business Modifications and Technical Modifications are performed, so that we can report correctly to the C-level.
  2. Clarification on the "Distance to Standard" card: What is it evaluating? We are unable to justify the CCLM numbers in Solman with the numbers from SAP4ME.

 

Thank you for your attention, and we look forward to your response.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: CCLM numbers in SAP for ME - Custom Code Analytics
entry.content:
Hi ,

Could you please check with the following?

1.Refer to KBA 2765131 to learn about how to start/use Custom Code Analytics.
2765131 - Getting Started with Custom Code Analytics based on SAP Solution Manager

2.Refer to KBA 3483840 to learn about how to understand Custom Code Analytics tiles. If you have an issue when opening the "Help" page, could you please report a new case to SV-SCS-CCM?
3483840 - Custom Code Analytics tiles explanation

Best regards,
Leo
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: How to Calculate the Total Value of Selected Pay Comp...
entry.content: Hello, could you please try to create a pay component group? A pay component group sum is the total amount (sum) of the recurring pay components that are part of a specific pay component group. Please refer to document: https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-employee-central/implementing-employee-compensation-data/optional-creating-pay-component-group

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP Custom entity referential constraint error wi...
entry.content:
From what I'm seeing, it feels like the RAP framework gets a bit tangled up when you try to rename key fields using @odata.property.name and have associations linking those keys between custom entities. It seems pretty good at renaming the property SESSION_ID to SessionId when just defining the entity itself in the metadata. But when it comes to automatically figuring out the ReferentialConstraint for the association I suspect it gets tripped up, maybe it's looking for the new name SessionId in the child based on the parent's key, but the actual link defined back in the CDS is still using the original ABAP field name.

Honestly, in my opinion the path of least resistance here is probably just to ditch the @odata.property.name. Since taking it off makes the error go away, it seems like the simplest fix to let the framework correctly wire up those constraints without getting confused by the renaming!

entry.title: Re: In SAP Public cloud -At the time of Asset scrap equip...
entry.content:
Hi Sudi,

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, Asset integration with Equipment is not supported.

Check below KBA and Blogs:

3196566 - Error 'Function is not available' appears when inputting a Main Asset Number in a Technical Object - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Missing the link of fixed asset with functional location or equipment

Equipment and Asset integration in cloud

This functionality is on roadmap and planned to be delivered in future, however no date can be communicated.

BR,

Nick

entry.content:
Hi @ST24,

Could you please check the storage type level config and product master-> Storage type , sorry i did not work with public could. Could see the documentation below and set up in your public cloud...let us know .

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: Is the Best practice for Master Data in S/4HANA Cloud...
entry.content: Thank you for your response and detailed guidance. After following the suggested steps in the SAP Signavio Process Navigator, I encountered some inconsistencies: 1XV – This scope item appears to be associated with SAP S/4HANA for Enterprise Contract Management rather than Master Data. 1YB –This scope item is linked to Import Connection Setup with SAP Analytics Cloud (1YB), which also seems unrelated to Master Data. 2LD – Unfortunately, I was unable to locate this scope item in the SAP Signavio Process Navigator. Could you please confirm if the mentioned scope items are correct for Master Data, or provide additional clarification? It would also be helpful if you could share specific steps or a direct link to access these Master Data Best Practices within the SAP Signavio Process Navigator.

entry.title: partition (1) contains *** records. A table partition cannot contain more than 2,147,483,64
entry.content:
The Table S*** has 2 partitions as we are reaching 2Billion mark, but we are observing that the Partition-1 of the the table is still increasing by few entries everyday. it is very close to 2Billion records and we would need suggestion on the house keeping for this table to keep the entries under the limit.Please find the screenshot of Historical Growth based on Daily Data: 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: partition (1) contains *** records. A table partition...
entry.content:
Review this note below and perform a reorg for large table as mentioned in note:

2374652 - Handling Very Large Data Volumes in SAP BW/4HANA
-> Handling Large Data Volumes - 2 Billion Limit Value Reached

As a remind that you need apply file TABLE_PLACEMENT.zip before the reorg as mentioned in this note.

entry.title: Re: Install on-premise partner add-ons for S/4HANA 2023 p...
entry.content:
Hi Prabhakaran, 

The add-on install is the same way mostly yes, but with some key differences. Private Cloud is technically very close to on-premise — it’s your system hosted on SAP or hyperscaler (AWS, Azure, GCP) but managed by SAP under RISE with SAP.

  • However, you (the customer/partner) don’t have direct system access like in pure on-premise. Everything must go through SAP’s operations teams.

Therefore:

On-Premise S/4HANA Private Cloud
You can install your add-on freelySAP has to install it for you (via service request)
You manage system upgradesSAP manages upgrades and validates add-ons
You can manually patch/add transport requestsYou must request SAP to import transports

Procedure to release Private Cloud-compatible add-ons

If you are a partner releasing add-ons for customers on Private Cloud:

  • Certification is mandatory. Your add-on must be SAP-certified for S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition (even if already certified for on-premise).

  • Certification is slightly different from standard on-premise certification.

    Steps:

    1. Check your add-on compatibility: Make sure it works on S/4HANA versions (like 2023, 2022 etc.).

    2. Work with SAP ICC (Integration and Certification Center): They will guide you on private cloud-specific certification.

      • You’ll likely need a PCE-specific test environment for this.

    3. Get your add-on listed in SAP’s certified Solution Directory.

  • Technically:

    • Your add-on must be delivered as SAINT/ADDON package (no manual steps ideally).

    • It must support zero-downtime upgrades where possible (important for PCE customers).

How customers install partner add-ons on Private Cloud

  • Customers cannot install it themselves.

  • Process:

    1. Customer raises a Service Request (SR) to SAP through their support channels (via SAP for Me or SAP Launchpad).

    2. Attach your certified add-on transport/package and documentation.

    3. SAP will perform the installation during agreed maintenance windows.

    4. Testing: You (partner/customer) must validate the add-on after SAP installs it.

There is no direct SE01, SAINT, SPAM access for customers in PCE!

If you like the answer then please mark it as answered and give me points.

entry.title: Re: gCTS is still here
entry.content: Hi @Karin, we have multiple customers (20+). which delivery method you will suggest? Multi-off Delivery or Scalable delivery?

entry.title: Re: 收入認列錯誤
entry.content:
HI Owen

我明白收入確認的相關科目最終會被Balance，但我指的是開票時不應該直接認列為收入，應該認為預收收入，根據IFRS15在責任移轉前不應該認列為實際收入科目，而是應該認列為負債類科目，如合約負債或遞延收入，待月底執行收入認列時，再根據完工百分比等方式衡量實際認列為收入的金額，您可以參考ByDesign的收入確認，我認為這樣才是合理且正確的收入認列科目轉換。

entry.title: Re: Log into new Client with SAP*
entry.content:
Hi Victor, 

Very simple 3 steps.

Step 1: Ask your DBA or if you are DBA then execute the SQL command

delete from USR02 where bname='SAP*' and mandt='101';

Step 2: Insert the login/no_automatic_user_sapstar = 0 in the instance profile. 

Step 3: Restart the application. DB restart not needed. 

Try password pass for sap* to login to the new client 101.

This will Solve your issue, please give kudos to my post if issue resolved.

entry.title: Re: Reusing Story Reports Across Annual Compensation Plan...

entry.title: Re: 收入認列錯誤
entry.content: 现在针对开票的收入确认就是在递延科目下面的，并不是实际收入。而且根据不同RA key可以定义收入确认的方法。可以实现根据完工百分比确认收入。

entry.title: Re: In S/4HANA Public Cloud, Can restrict user from change the approved PR?

Ability to report all Compensation forms at once via story reports

entry.title: Re: In S/4HANA Public Cloud, Can restrict user from chang...

On another note, I forwarded the PR for one of the approval steps, the original user is back and approved the PR from his inbox. Now, the workflow has initiated again despite the PO is already created.

entry.title: Re: Adding People Profile link in Forms
entry.content:
@PrinceThomas 

As KBA 3483860 - How to create deep link for People profile block sections? 

Deeplinks to blocks is not available yet in the Latest People Profile (PPX) as per document Differences Between Legacy and Latest People Profile | SAP Help Portal. Once it is available, the KBA will be updated. 

Hope it help!

James

entry.title: Re: 收入認列錯誤
entry.content: HI Owen 我說的認列應該認列在預收收入底下指的是文檔https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/89d896ca9cd64318b1667df5ec00e4b2/23cee4ab4c5443c6b68e274e69dcf19f.html?locale=zh-CN 中，提到的第二步，開票時的收入科目您可以很清楚的看到收入科目是直接認列的，並沒有先進到遞延才透過收入確認運行轉出，透過收入確認轉出的是過度科目的抵銷，但開票還是直接認列的收入科目，這樣是不對的，另外我知道可以透過完工百分比來進行，例如EPMFC或EPMCC謝謝。

entry.title: Transportation Management 端到端流程演示-2502
entry.content:
业务场景

总部位于都柏林的创新爱尔兰公司（Innovate Ireland）与德国汉堡的零售商TechnoWelt达成了一项重要协议，将向TechnoWelt供应智能家居设备。经过仔细评估并考虑成本等因素后,他们决定采用海运方式。这意味着他们必须预订集装箱,并且要规划、执行和跟踪将货物运输和交付给客户的整个流程。

第一阶段：港口装货，第二阶段：海运，最后阶段：卸货港至客户所在地

系统演示

请查看以下链接视频

Transportation Management 端到端流程演示 

感谢观看

entry.title: Re: 收入認列錯誤
entry.content: 如果您觉得这个设计有待改善，请提出Feature Request。

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors story table issues
entry.content:
Hi @MikeC 

Story report issues for me boiled down to configuration & RBP. 

You can check 3050294 - Stories in People Analytics - Known Issues 

For configuration, sometime you need to run provisioning job. Following guide of 3042974 - Schemas missing in Story 

"MDF Object Definition To DB SYNC" and "Save Changes To Report Configuration Object Job" (make sure to check box for Custom Schema) jobs need to be ran respectively:

  1. Go to Provisioning and go to the affected company
  2. Search for Clear cache under MDF Cache Tool and then run it
  3. Next, run the job "MDF Object Definition To DB SYNC". ( can be run from Admin Center - See below Note)
  4. Once this is completed, create a new job "Save Changes To Report Configuration Object Job", run this and wait for it to be completed.
  5. Log in to BizX environment and previously missing schemas are available now.

Hope it help!

James

entry.title: Enterprise Architecture as a Leadership Factor
entry.content:
Last week at DSAG [1] Technology Days in Wiesbaden, I had the privilege of delivering two sessions that promoted SAP’s thought leadership in enterprise architecture. The enthusiastic response from both the CIO Circle [2] and general attendees reinforced the growing strategic importance of architecture in driving digital transformation. The presentation followed why, what, and how sections.

WHY Enterprise Architecture Matters for Leadership

Our first session, “Enterprise Architecture as a Leadership Factor” presented alongside @helmut_grimm to the DSAG CIO Circle, addressed a fundamental challenge voiced by many IT leaders: “If what we do in IT doesn’t move the business forward, it simply doesn’t matter.”

As our conversation candidly put it, “Will this application help us sell more [for example] bread rolls?” This question gets to the heart of IT value delivery. Enterprise architecture serves as the critical bridge that:

  • Elevates leadership from local decision-making to enterprise-wide optimization
  • Provides the holistic perspective needed for strategic business transformation
  • Breaks down siloed thinking by promoting cross-functional architectural vision
  • Creates a unified leadership language connecting business strategy and technology execution

WHAT Enterprise Architecture Delivers as a Leadership Factor

In this session, we articulated enterprise architecture not merely as a technical framework but as a comprehensive leadership discipline that shapes organizational strategy:

Enterprise architecture as a discipline provides the structured approach to aligning business vision with IT execution through consistent frameworks, models, and principles that govern organizational design decisions. As a leadership factor, EA transcends its traditional role to become:

  • A strategic lens that transforms how leaders perceive organizational challenges—shifting from fragmented tactical responses to holistic architectural solutions
  • An enterprise-wide optimization mechanism that elevates decision-making beyond departmental silos to address ecosystem-wide opportunities and constraints
  • A governance framework that enables leaders to evaluate initiatives based on their enterprise-wide impact rather than localized benefits
  • A communication bridge connecting business vision with technical implementation through architectural models that create shared understanding
  • A balancing force that reconciles competing priorities by establishing clear design principles and evaluation criteria

The evolution of EA from technical documentation to leadership competency represents a shift in how organizations approach transformation—moving from project-focused implementations to capability-driven architecture.

HOW Leaders Can Adopt Enterprise Architecture

We provided attendees with six practical techniques for adopting EA as a leadership approach:

  1. ORCHESTRATION: Strategic alignment of people, processes, and technology across organizational boundaries
  2. MODERNIZATION: Driving business transformation through architectural patterns
  3. SIMPLIFICATION: Creating business clarity through deliberate design choices
  4. BUSINESS-ORIENTATION: Shifting from managing systems to shaping business outcomes
  5. STRUCTURATION: Gaining structure and tools to drive complex change
  6. DECISION: Acquiring clarity to act decisively based on real business priorities

Impact and Response

The CIO Circle session generated exceptional engagement from senior IT leaders across industries. The intimate setting with C-level participants created a candid exchange about leadership challenges that resonated deeply with attendees.

Several CIOs approached us after the presentation to share how the framework helped them recontextualize struggles they had been facing. One automotive technology leader remarked: “We’ve invested heavily in EA tools but missed the leadership mindset shift—this explains why we haven’t seen the strategic outcomes we expected.”

Particularly impactful was our discussion of EA as an orchestration mechanism. Multiple attendees expressed interest in implementing the decision model that evaluates impacts across organizational boundaries, with several requesting follow-up workshops for their leadership teams. The concept of architectural thinking as a leadership competency rather than just a technical skill generated the most questions during our extended Q&A.

The DSAG CIO Advisory Board has subsequently requested a dedicated deep-dive session on establishing governance mechanisms that promote global perspective over local optimization.

For colleagues engaging with senior IT leadership, the EA leadership techniques offer valuable conversation starters to elevate discussions beyond technical implementation to strategic architectural thinking. For those interested in learning more, I’m happy to share the presentation materials below or discuss how we can incorporate these insights into our customer engagements.

Special thanks to Helmut Grimm for his excellent collaboration, and to the DSAG organization for providing this platform to showcase SAP’s thought leadership in enterprise architecture.

by Dr. Michael R. Blaschke

[1] The DSAG is one of the most influential SAP user associations in the world. Many dedicated members from over 4,000 companies form a strong network: from medium-sized businesses to DAX-listed corporations, from the specialist department to the CxO level, across all industries, in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland (DACH). The user association is organized into 200 working groups and committees.

[2] The DSAG CIO Circle and its elected CIO Advisory Board are committees within the DSAG organization that exclusively include IT decision-makers from SAP user companies. This ensures that those who can assess and demand solutions from a broader perspective—and ultimately make the business decisions—are represented. The CIO Circle is open to CIOs and heads of corporate governance from SAP user companies.

entry.title: Re: What is the main function for 'Item Cateogry' in the...
entry.content:
Dear Edrilan,

GOT it. Thanks a lot!

Vincent

entry.title: Centralized vs. Distributed AI Strategies for Enterprises: A Comparative Study
entry.content:
In the dynamic world of enterprise AI, choosing the right strategy can be the key to unlocking unprecedented innovation and efficiency. Dive into our comparative study of centralized and distributed AI strategies to discover how leading companies are navigating this critical decision, leveraging cutting-edge SAP tools, and transforming stakeholder experiences. Whether you're an IT professional, business leader, end-user, or customer, this exploration offers valuable insights to propel your AI journey forward. Curious to learn more? Let's delve into the details!

Understanding stakeholder perspectives is crucial for successful AI adaptation in enterprises running on SAP. Building the stakeholder perspectives on AI adaptation in an enterprise running on SAP is very important in developing the AI roadmap. The four key stakeholders are:

Business Leaders: Business leaders prioritize strategic alignment and ROI. They are concerned with Value Realization, Decision-Making and Innovation.
Business Users or End-Users: End-users focus on usability and impact on daily operations. Their perspectives include Ease of Use, Productivity, Business Function and Process Support.
Customers: Customers are vital stakeholders whose experiences and satisfaction directly impact business success. Their perspectives include Personalization, Efficiency, Trust and Transparency and Feedback Integration.
Business IT functions: IT teams are crucial in implementing and maintaining AI solutions. They focus on Operations Stability, Application Lifecycles, Integration, Scalability and Security.

To address these varied needs and drive effective AI integration we have various SAP Products and Tools for AI Adaptation including SAP AI Core, SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Generative AI Hub, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Customer Data Cloud, SAP Service Cloud and various SAP embedded Business AI solutions. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) allows to consume almost any external AI models through a robust API integration, thus it is not necessary for Enterprise to adapt only SAP provided AI solutions but to adapt whatever suits best and at their own speed.

In the rapidly evolving landscape of artificial intelligence (AI), SAP enterprise architects face the critical decision of choosing between centralized and distributed AI strategies. This comparative study explores the benefits, drawbacks, adaptation, value, and governance of both approaches that can be used as a guide for building such roadmaps. Do find the comparison here.

 

 

Centralized AI Strategy

Distributed AI Strategy

Benefits

Consistency and Cohesion: Ensures alignment with organizational objectives and values. For example, corporates can centralized its AI strategy to streamline firm-wide technology consumption and foster the integration of innovative solutions.

Resource Optimization: Prevents duplication of efforts and reduces costs. Centralized AI decisioning hub can significantly enhanced customer service and reduced churn.

Knowledge Sharing: Facilitates sharing of insights, data, and expertise across departments. Enterprise transformation into an AI-centric organization involved strategic restructuring and a shift to cloud and consolidated solutions in one common service platform.

Unified Governance: Provides robust governance structures to manage ethical and compliance issues. Corporate need to emphasize on strong governance to mitigate risks associated with AI.

Enhanced Scalability: Can handle complex problems by dividing tasks among multiple agents. Businesses can leveraged multiple AI agent system to improve product configuration workflow and customer satisfaction.

Improved Fault Tolerance: If one node fails, others can take over its tasks.

Efficient Resource Utilization: Maximizes the potential of interconnected devices.

Faster Innovation: Enables rapid prototyping and closer alignment with business needs. Google DeepMind's AlphaFold project significantly accelerated drug discovery and disease understanding. Business can adapt any AI model that suits their need for faster innovation or time to market.

 

 

Drawbacks

Single Point of Failure: If the central system fails, it can disrupt the entire AI operation.

Scalability Issues: May struggle to handle large-scale data and complex computations.

Potential Bias: Centralized systems may reflect the biases of the development team.

Privacy Risks: Concentrating data makes the system vulnerable to privacy breaches.

Communication Overhead: Managing communication between nodes can be challenging.

Synchronization Issues: Ensuring synchronization across distributed systems can be complex.

Data Collection Challenges: Overburdens networks and generates latency issues.

Security Risks: Distributed systems can be vulnerable to security breaches.

Adaptation

AI Center of Excellence (CoE): Establishing an AI CoE can streamline initiatives, reduce duplication, and focus on projects with significant business results.

Embedded AI: Adapt AI technology in enterprise platform.

Usage Focused: Business is investing in AI to run their business, and not to learn AI. Build business role and stakeholder centric use cases.

Edge AI: Leveraging edge AI technologies for real-time adaptation and autonomous operation.

Hybrid Model: Combining centralized foundations with decentralized innovation with published charters for all stakeholders for managing the AI archetypes.

Digital Transformation: Bridging digital transformation and AI integration in your day to day activities and digital journeys.

Value

Competitive Advantage: Provides essential focus and direction, driving competitive advantage.

Accelerates Value Realization: Unified strategy helps in faster realization of AI benefits.

Reduces Wasteful Spending: Optimizes resource allocation and minimizes project failure risks.

Operational Efficiency: Enhances system reliability, performance, and scalability.

Business Strategy Alignment: Helps in aligning AI deployment with strategic goals.

Competitive Edge: Provides a framework for critical AI decisions to deliver tangible value.

Governance

Centralized Governance Model: Implementing controls and accountability structures to monitor AI ethics, data privacy, and security.

Regulation and Oversight: Developing clear guidelines and regulations to ensure ethical and responsible use. 

Decentralized Governance: Promoting decentralized AI systems to distribute power and innovation.

Regulation and Oversight: Developing clear guidelines and regulations to ensure ethical and responsible use.

 

The future of AI is marked by several key trends, including the rise of generative AI, multimodal AI, and AI augmentation in the workforce. These advancements will shape how enterprises implement and govern AI strategies, emphasizing the need for adaptability and forward-thinking approaches.

Understanding stakeholder perspectives is crucial for successful AI implementation. Stakeholders such as IT professionals, business leaders, and end-users have varying priorities and concerns, from data privacy to operational efficiency. Engaging stakeholders in the decision-making process ensures that AI strategies align with organizational goals and address potential challenges effectively.

Both centralized and distributed AI strategies offer unique benefits and challenges. Centralized AI provides consistency, resource optimization, and robust governance, but may face scalability and privacy issues. Distributed AI enhances scalability, fault tolerance, and innovation, but requires careful management of communication and synchronization. Enterprises should consider their specific needs, resources, and goals to choose the most suitable strategy, potentially adopting a hybrid approach to leverage the strengths of both models.

entry.title: What's New in SAP Reference Architecture? [Spring Edition]
entry.content:
​For those of you, who need a refresher on what SAP has in store for Enterprise Architects: Along with Tooling, Methodology and two other elements, which define our Enterprise Architecture Framework, SAP also provides Reference Architectures. The SAP Reference Architecture is a standardized framework developed to align business processes with IT solutions, facilitating effective business transformations. It comprises two main components:​

  • SAP Reference Business Architecture (RBA): RBA offers a business-centric and product-agnostic perspective of an enterprise. It organizes the enterprise into four enterprise domains—Products & Services, Supply, Customer, and Corporate—each encompassing specific business capabilities. Additionally, the RBA outlines a comprehensive set of business process variants for different industries and business models. These are based on eight generic end-to-end business processes, such as Idea to Market, Source to Pay, and Lead to Cash. ​
  • SAP Reference Solution Architecture (RSA): Building upon the RBA, the RSA illustrates how SAP's product portfolio addresses various business challenges. It provides detailed solution architecture recommendations, including process information, to guide implementations and customer extensions. ​

Together, these components enable organizations to understand and implement SAP solutions effectively, ensuring alignment between business objectives and IT strategies.

Recently, the Capability Model of the SAP Reference Architecture has been extended towards Technology Capabilities. In addition to that, also a first wave of Business Data Objects, which is particularly important in the context of SAP Business Data Cloud were included. Both, the Technology Capability Model along with Business Data Objects will be the focus of this article.

Hint: If you are new to the world of Reference Content and Enterprise Architecture at SAP, we strongly recommend to check out this blog post by Maria Goltz here. SAP Reference Architecture is one part of the broader SAP Enterprise Enterprise Architecture Framework. Learn more about it in Rene de Daniel’s post here.

 

Technology Capability Model

Purpose: Why Technology Capabilities?

The Business Capability Model (BCM), currently included in the SAP Reference Architecture covers a full set all of capabilities required to perform business activities within an organization. What is not in scope of the BCM is generic, supporting capabilities that are not directly related to specific business contexts, but that may provide basic technology support or enhancements for business capabilities. Yet, SAP’s product offering comprises both: business applications that are directly related to specific business contexts, as well as technology components that enable generic technology capabilities.

To address both aspects of our offering and our customers’ needs, there is a clear expectation by the market and need for SAP to also provide a Technology Capability Model as part of SAP’s Reference architecture. Since early 2025, the Technology Capability Model (TCM) has been made available as part of the RBA/RSA.

 

Definition: What are Technology Capabilities?

Technology Capabilities describe generic abilities that are provided by technology and are relevant for an organization - independent of a concrete solution and independent from their application in a potential or actual business context. This differentiates them from Business Capabilities, which are always tied to a specific functional area of the business.

While Business Capabilities typically describe an organization’s basic capacity to perform Business Activities, Technology Capabilities can complement Business Capabilities to address underlying technological needs and/or enable additional functionality that is not part of Business Capabilities themselves.

Entities in the Technology Capability Model are typically defined “outside in”, i.e. independent of SAP offering, product structure, organization setup or strategic priorities. The Technology Capability Model follows the same hierarchical structure as the RBA Business Capability Model (RBA BCM). Each top level “Technology Domain” is broken down into several Technology Areas. Currently, there are six Technology Domains defined for the TCM:

  • Application Development and Automation
  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Data and Analytics
  • Integration
  • IT Operations
  • IT Security

Technology Areas are again broken down into several Technology Capabilities. A Technology Capability can be implemented by one or several Tech Solution Capabilities, each of which is enabled with a different set of one or several Technology Solution Components.

Use cases: How can I work with the Technology Capability Model?

The Technology Capability Model serves many purposes and can be beneficial in multiple situations. The following three phases serve as examples to depict the value of TCM. If you are eager to explore TCM, you can do so by checking out this spreadsheet. It is currently evaluated, in which form the TCM will be made available in SAP LeanIX moving forward.

  • Begin: As the TCM is defined in a generic fashion, regardless of business context and provider-specifics, the Enterprise Architect can adopt a neutral, outside-in perspective. Start with a mapping of generic abilities that are provided by technology and are relevant for an organization. Then identify, if and which specific SAP solutions are available to enable the capability.
  • Extend: With the Technology Capability Model the scope of Reference Architecture is extended, allowing you to not only focus on business context but also on technology features. If you have already, and exhaustively modelled all business capabilities, you can now continue with technology capabilities.  As opposed to BCM, the TCM is modelled independent of SAP offerings, product structure, organization setup or strategic priorities. This allows you to capture additional technological features and enrich your deliverable. With both models, TCM and BCM at your fingertips, you can convey SAP’s offering as a provider of both Business Applications and Technology Platform and underline how these complement each other.
  • Focus: Apart from starting and enriching existing capability maps, you can also leverage the TCM to drill down into specific focus areas. Navigate through the various capabilities Technology can offer and learn details about these! One common example is to detect potential for Artificial Intelligence within a business.

 

Business Data Objects

Apart from TCM, SAP Reference Architecture now also covers a first wave of SAP Business Data Objects (BDO). Not sure what these are? One can think of SAP Business Data Objects as structured representations of business entities (like customers, orders, or invoices) to model, store, and manage business data. They play a key part in organizing and processing information. In brief, Business Data Objects:

  • Represent business entities or concepts (e.g. Sales Order).
  • Are used to support data integration, reporting, transactions, and process automation.

Within SAP, BDO’s can show up in SAP S/4HANA and Fiori apps for example. For now, a first set of business data objects for cross-industry processes has been mapped in the SAP Reference Architecture. Going forward, even more Business Data Objects will be included.

Objectives of BDO in SAP Reference Architecture

Now, one might wonder why Business Data Objects are included in the SAP Reference Business Architecture. So far, the SAP Reference Architecture had a focus on processes and capabilities. Surely, SAP’s strategy for 2025 with focus on Business Data Cloud is one aspect, why Business Data Objects have been added to the roadmap of the SAP Reference Architecture: With SAP’s focus on unifying data in the SAP Business Data Cloud (SAP BDC), a clear focus on business data, including business data objects, becomes clear. SAP BDC is one of SAP's newest offerings in the realm of data unification, semantic modeling, and real-time business intelligence. SAP BDC is a unified data foundation that allows organizations to access, integrate, model, and analyze business data across SAP and non-SAP systems, while preserving its business meaning and context.

By bringing in BDO’s into the SAP Reference Architecture, our aim is to meet our customer’s and consumers needs: When digging into the realm of data, Enterprise Architects using SAP Reference Architecture need a consistent taxonomy across processes to define data distribution. Having a consistent taxonomy available allows for a shared language between business and IT and to bridge transactional and analytical worlds.

BDO’s provided in SAP Reference Architecture are closely aligned with SAP’s data offerings and provide the required context. This helps Enterprise Architects overcome application specific names and segmentation challenges.

Interested in learning more about SAP Business Data Cloud? You might want to check out Gaurish Dessai’s blog post about Business Data Cloud here

 

Moving forward with BDO in SAP Reference Architecture

Currently, a first set of business data objects for cross-industry processes is included in the SAP Reference Architecture. Soon, Business Data Objects will be made availability on SAP LeanIX and SAP Signavio Process Explorer. A larger scope of BDO’s in the SAP Reference Architecture, and making it available to broader audiences paves the way for the following benefits:

  • Data as strategic asset: Data is a strategic asset that improves decision-making and optimizes operations, helping organizations gain a competitive advantage. SAP Customers, EAs and Partners will be able to get a deeper understanding of the Data Architecture and derive benefits within RISE or GROW with SAP Journeys.
  • Business context:BDOs are and remain highly relevant in the context of SAP Business Data Cloud: They show how data is modeled, managed, and consumed across different systems and provide the respective semantic and business context. Business Data Objects do not handle data as raw tables, fields, or technical structures, instead, data is understood and organized in terms of what it means to the business and how it is used in business processes.

  • Increased transparency on data consumption: Once available in SAP LeanIX, users working with SAP Reference Architecture can easily build and extend their inventory of Data Objects mapped to the respective SAP Applications. Example: by mapping the relationship of a Business Data Object to a Business Capability and the Application Factsheet, users can easily learn which application uses which data. Additionally, Business Data Objects create the foundation for users to create diagrams visualizing the flow of data between applications effectively.

  • Industry-specifics: Once made available in SAP LeanIX, users will be able to select an industry and identify industry-specific architectures for Public Cloud and Hybrid deployments, including the respective Business Data Objects. This allows for an even clearer view on Data Architectures, taking into account the various industry-specifics.

entry.title: Re: Mapping task Mapping of Text ID contains invalid valu...
entry.content: Thank you : ) !

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Need APIs for Automating F...
entry.content:
Hi,

You can check if the following APIs meet the business requirements:

Overview | Journal Entry - Post (Asynchronous) | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Business documentation: Journal Entry - Post (Asynchronous) | SAP Help Portal

Best regards,

Sharon

entry.title: Re: Move SD basic shipment to Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

Please check transport management scope items . 

External Transportation Planning (6W1)

Manual Transportation Planning (6W2)
Transportation Execution (6W3)
 
Regards
Sahil

entry.title: Re: 收入認列錯誤
entry.content: HI Owen 在哪提交Feature Request?如何提交Feature Request呢?

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Challenge March - SAP Community Escape House - AI 🤖🧠
entry.content:
Hi All

I'm trying to do the AI escape room but I'm not sure how to run the python scripts as they need the service-key.json file which I assume is the instance service key from AI Core BTP service but the service is not available in BTP trial. 

Was there a sandbox instance provided while the challenge was still active?

Can this challenge still be done now or am I too late to the party?

Thanks

entry.title: Is there any replacement for sap side effect?
entry.content: Is there any solution to do refresh without using side effect, i want to this because my sap version can't use side effect

entry.title: Re: Error "Unable to recognize the artifact type [notific...
entry.content:
Hi @Giang1

Looks more of an issue from the product itself. I quickly checked internally and face the same issue. I would suggest raising a SAP incident so the relevant support team can pick it up for further investigation.

Kind Regards,

Karthik

entry.title: Re: 收入認列錯誤
entry.content: See ##2963059 How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product The link for Influence for finance is https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/1169

entry.title: Schedule Contact Email tag in SystemEnrollmentNotification not working
entry.content:
Hi

I am trying to add the 'Schedule Contact Email' (Standard Tag) to the SystemEnrollmentNotification in SuccessFactors Learning but this tag is displaying the global application admin email and not the one that is added within Class > Contacts. 

Note: Schedule Contact is picking up with no issues.

Raised a ticket with SAP but they came back saying custom emails is not supported. Considering this is a standard tag, I'd assume this must work with no issues.

Has anyone come across the same issue and have any suggestions please?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Is there any replacement for sap side effect?
entry.content: if you can't define it at backend, you can define it at frontend. it is same thing

entry.title: Re: gCTS is still here
entry.content:
Hi,
the recommendation is to use Scalable in this case. Multi-Off is recommended for up to ten customers

Kind regards
Karin

entry.title: Re: I am facing an error in retrieving IBD in EWM, error message number - SR053 - An exception has o
